description: Sources behind the two published Public Source stories on the Pittsburgh Water board, and the peer comparison. Each 'file' source is fetched, content-validated, and hashed by capture.py; each 'citation' source is a live URL archived to the Wayback Machine (statutes, press releases, and the published stories themselves, which are not re-hosted here).

sources.title: Pittsburgh Water board minutes and agendas (24 meetings, Oct 2023 - Sep 2025)
sources.note: The source of record for every meeting-level figure. The 24 individual meeting-minute PDFs are the next archiving step; the master coding of them lives in 02-data/ and reproduces via build.py.

sources.title: Niagara Falls Water Board business meeting minutes, April 28, 2025
sources.note: Peer exemplar: employee-evaluation resolution failed 2-2, was revised in public, passed 3-2; 161-minute meeting.

sources.title: North Harris County Regional Water Authority executed minutes, October 7, 2024
sources.note: Peer exemplar: motion failed 2-2 with two abstentions; 3-2 to postpone; 179-minute meeting, 9 speakers, 35-minute executive session. The authority restructured its uploads path and the original PDF URL now 404s with no Wayback snapshot; relocating the executed minutes is an open archiving item. Niagara Falls, the peer the story leads with, is captured and hashed.

sources.title: OCC Comptroller's Handbook - Bank Supervision Process (board effectiveness guidance)
sources.note: Approval rates approaching 99-100% may indicate a board has ceased providing active oversight and independent judgment.

sources.title: Kennesaw State analysis of 848 Enron board votes, 1997-2001
sources.note: Enron board approved 99.5% of resolutions unanimously; a questioning board can still be a rubber stamp.

sources.title: RAND perspective on Pittsburgh Water lead remediation options (2017)
sources.note: Prior work by interviewed source Linnea Warren Mears.

sources.title: Pennsylvania Sunshine Act, 65 Pa.C.S. Chapter 7
sources.note: Sections 706 and 708(c) cited for the public-deliberation requirement and the executive-session subterfuge prohibition.

sources.title: Pennsylvania NewsMedia Association guide to the Sunshine Act
PA PUC press release on the Pittsburgh Water rate case (July 10, 2025)
sources.note: Rate case filed June 4, 2025; decision due March 8, 2026.

sources.title: Two years, zero 'no' votes on Pittsburgh Water board as rate hike looms (Public Source)
sources.note: The published baseline story. Byline Brian Nuckols.

sources.title: Pittsburgh Water board asks questions after transparency investigation (Public Source)
sources.note: The observer-effect follow-up. Byline Brian Nuckols.
